---
title: "Longitudinal Dataset Creation"
output: html_document
---

# Longitudinal test for CFAR Social Networks

Check-ins are at 6/12/18/24mo periods

### Preliminaries 

```{r echo = T, results = "hide", message = F, warnings = F}
library(tidyr) # data cleaning processing 
library(dplyr) # further data manipulation (%>% (pipe) operator)
library(igraph) # social networks 
library(RColorBrewer) # color palettes 
library(stringr) # regex 
library(purrr) # used to create the network objects 
```


### Data Preparation

We want to be able to stratify by age at enrollment, HEU/HUU, and both together 

- We will then repeat analysis (metrics) at eat time component 
- But first, we will focus on visualization 
- Already have proven that the set.seed() function allows the algorithm to deterministically keep the clusters in the same positions in Igraph plots
- The cleanest way to do this is just to create new datasets at each timepoint and plot from those... to avoid double counting

```{r}
raw_data <- read.csv("/Users/maxschultz/CFAR-SN-Project/Datasets/CHERISHSN_DATA_2026-03-24_1747(in).csv")
#head(raw_data)
```

treat each arm as its own timepoint, delivery_arm_3 is enrolled at delivery and the prefix for the rest delineates the chek-in time. cfar_arm_1 are people who were enrolled in the cross-sectional portion only (n=15) but if they have a csn_k_id then they are part of the longitudinal analysis and timepoint is cfar_v_timepoint_c - information is later under their k ids 


delete all cfars lol -- This was the final determination in the last meeting with Dr. Campbell

15 in total deleting from the longitudinal - this was actually determined to be 30 

```{r}
# number of cfars deleting from the data
length(unique(raw_data$record_id[which(raw_data$redcap_event_name == "cfar_arm_1")]))
```

So there are actually 30 unique index patients who are being 'removed' from the longitudinal analysis.

Unfortunately, index-level data is stored in cfar_arm_1 so we will need to calculate them separately and then merge the two datasets. 

Here is the code for just the contact-level data population for the longitudinal analysis. 

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# ============================================================
# CONTACT-LEVEL LONGITUDINAL SOCIAL NETWORK DATASET ONLY
# ============================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Base longitudinal rows
# -----------------------------

base_long <- raw_data %>%
  filter(redcap_event_name != "cfar_arm_1") %>%
  mutate(
    record_id = as.character(record_id),
    timepoint = case_when(
      redcap_event_name == "delivery_arm_3" ~ "0m",
      redcap_event_name == "6_month_arm_3" ~ "6m",
      redcap_event_name == "12_month_arm_3" ~ "12m",
      redcap_event_name == "12_month_k23c2_arm_4" ~ "12m",
      redcap_event_name == "18_month_k23c2_arm_4" ~ "18m",
      redcap_event_name == "24_month_k23c2_arm_4" ~ "24m",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(timepoint))


# -----------------------------
# 2. Basic contact variables
# -----------------------------
# These are stored as:
# snml_sex_1, snml_vage_1, snml_relate_1, hh_id_1, etc.

basic_contacts <- base_long %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches(
      "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_\\d+$"
    ),
    names_to = c(".value", "ctc_idx"),
    names_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_(\\d+)$",
    values_drop_na = FALSE
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ctc_idx = as.integer(ctc_idx),
    contact_visit_id = str_c("ctc_", record_id, "_tp_", timepoint, "_idx_", ctc_idx)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !(is.na(snml_sex) & is.na(snml_vage) & is.na(snml_relate) & is.na(hh_id))
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    record_id = as.character(record_id),
    ctc_idx = as.integer(ctc_idx),
    contact_visit_id,
    timepoint = as.character(timepoint),

    snml_vage_mrg = as.numeric(snml_vage),
    snml_sex_mrg = factor(snml_sex),
    snml_relate_mrg = factor(snml_relate),
    hh_id_unified_ = factor(hh_id),

    pcare_mrg = factor(pcare),
    smoke_mrg = factor(smoke),
    drug_mrg = factor(drug),
    tb_dx_mrg = factor(tb_dx),
    room_mrg = factor(room),
    bed_mrg = factor(bed)
  )


# -----------------------------
# 3. Helper function for exposure variables
# -----------------------------
# These are checkbox variables stored as:
# 0m:  lived_1___1
# 6m:  lived_1_6m___1
# 12m: lived_1_12m___1
# 18m: lived_1_18m___1
# 24m: lived_1_24m___1

make_exposure <- function(data, tp, suffix_pattern, names_pattern) {
  data %>%
    filter(timepoint == tp) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = matches(suffix_pattern),
      names_to = c(".value", "ctc_idx"),
      names_pattern = names_pattern,
      values_drop_na = FALSE
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      record_id = as.character(record_id),
      ctc_idx = as.integer(ctc_idx),
      timepoint = as.character(tp)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      record_id,
      ctc_idx,
      timepoint,
      lived_mrg = as.integer(lived),
      stayed_mrg = as.integer(stayed),
      visited_mrg = as.integer(visited),
      occas_mrg = as.integer(occas),
      elswe_mrg = as.integer(elswe)
    )
}


# -----------------------------
# 4. Exposure contact variables
# -----------------------------

exposure_contacts <- bind_rows(
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "0m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)___1$"
  ),
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "6m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+_6m___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)_6m___1$"
  ),
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "12m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+_12m___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)_12m___1$"
  ),
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "18m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+_18m___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)_18m___1$"
  ),
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "24m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+_24m___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)_24m___1$"
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    record_id = as.character(record_id),
    ctc_idx = as.integer(ctc_idx),
    timepoint = as.character(timepoint)
  )


# -----------------------------
# 5. Final contact-level dataset
# -----------------------------

df_contacts <- basic_contacts %>%
  left_join(
    exposure_contacts,
    by = c("record_id", "ctc_idx", "timepoint")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    timepoint = factor(timepoint, levels = c("0m", "6m", "12m", "18m", "24m"))
  ) %>%
  select(
    record_id,
    contact_visit_id,
    timepoint,

    snml_vage_mrg,
    snml_sex_mrg,
    snml_relate_mrg,
    hh_id_unified_,

    pcare_mrg,
    smoke_mrg,
    drug_mrg,
    tb_dx_mrg,

    lived_mrg,
    stayed_mrg,
    visited_mrg,
    occas_mrg,
    elswe_mrg,

    room_mrg,
    bed_mrg
  )


# -----------------------------
# 6. Benchmark checks
# -----------------------------

df_contacts %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_index_cases = n_distinct(record_id),
    missing_contact_age = sum(is.na(snml_vage_mrg)),
    missing_contact_sex = sum(is.na(snml_sex_mrg)),
    missing_contact_relationship = sum(is.na(snml_relate_mrg)),
    missing_household_id = sum(is.na(hh_id_unified_)),
    missing_lived = sum(is.na(lived_mrg)),
    missing_stayed = sum(is.na(stayed_mrg)),
    missing_visited = sum(is.na(visited_mrg)),
    missing_occas = sum(is.na(occas_mrg)),
    missing_elswe = sum(is.na(elswe_mrg))
  )

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, lived_mrg)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, stayed_mrg)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, visited_mrg)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, occas_mrg)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, elswe_mrg)
head(df_contacts, 20)
```

Up until here the dataset is populated with contact-level information. Now we have to focus on index-level attributes. To make it easier, we will just create a smaller dataset with the following specific attributes and then merge: 

- enrol_age: enrollment age
- cherish_sn_study_group: HUU/HEU status 
- final_igra_result
- etc.
